heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: A relentless rainstorm took place on 09 November 2025, it lasted 144.8 hours and it delivered 47.89mm of rain at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Currently no stormy weather.  The most recent storm happened on 02 January 2026 and it lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Right now, a cold snap is in effect, it started on 2026-01-01 and has continued for {days} day{plurals}.  There has been 1 cold snap this year; usually, 3.6 occurs annually.

extreme.status: The maximum temperature difference seen is 26.7°C, observed on 29 April 2025 when the temperature fell to 0.4°C but also managed to reach 27.1°C on the same day.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 0 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (198°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: 1737 miles is the largest wind run recorded on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: 93.7 mph is the largest wind speed recorded on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 6.4°C), outside temperature (down 2.1°C), and feels like temperature (down 2.1°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 226 m).

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 226.4m lower than yesterday, 74.8m lower than the past week, 61.4m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 853m to a maximum of 2220m, averaging 1042.3m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1117.1m and 1103.7m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: Cloud cover will be variable, overcast at times.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should feel pleasantly mild. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, near calm conditions. Possible light showers, cloud cover will be variable, overcast at times, and low sunlight due to cloudy or overcast skies. Low pressure may bring unsettled weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (1.5°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
